text.title: structured-output-basics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for structured-output-basics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Passing JSON Mode output to downstream code without schema validation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: JSON Mode guarantees parseable JSON, not that required keys exist or values are the right type. Missing keys cause KeyError in the consumer.

failure-mode.description: JSON Mode (OpenAI, Anthropic) guarantees valid JSON but NOT schema compliance — downstream validation is still required
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Valid JSON does not mean required keys are present or values match the schema.

failure-mode.description: OpenAI Structured Outputs (beta.parse) is gpt-4o family only; not available for o1/o3 reasoning models
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Fall back to function-forcing pattern for unsupported models.

failure-mode.description: Claude has no native Structured Outputs equivalent — must use prompt engineering + json.loads with retry
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Claude has tool use but no parsed response type like OpenAI.

failure-mode.description: Deeply nested schemas with many optional fields produce inconsistent results even with Structured Outputs
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Model accuracy degrades with nesting depth.

failure-mode.description: Very large schemas (>50 fields) increase prompt token cost and reduce model accuracy
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Context and model capacity are both limited.

failure-mode.description: Pydantic validators run client-side after parsing — API errors and validation errors need separate handling
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Schema constraints like ge=0 are not enforced by the LLM itself.

failure-mode.description: response.choices[0].message.parsed is None when the model refuses to generate structured output (content policy)
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always check for None before accessing.

failure-mode.description: OpenAI Structured Outputs are not supported for streaming + parse simultaneously (as of 2026)
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Choose one — either streaming OR parse.

failure-mode.description: Claude sometimes wraps JSON in ```json ``` even when told not to — always strip markdown code fences before parsing
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Markdown wrapping is not fully controlled by prompts.

failure-mode.description: instructor library adds retry overhead but can mask model failures; set a max retry limit and surface errors explicitly
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Retries can hide the real problem when limits are too high.

failure-mode.description: Large schemas (>20 fields) increase prompt tokens significantly — measure token cost before shipping complex extraction pipelines
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Token cost scales with schema complexity.
